title: I Turned My Backyard Pool Into a Lap Pool (Sort Of)
gist: A product review of iGarden's $3,599 Swim Jet X Pro 10, a battery-powered current-generating device that turns a small backyard pool into a resistance-swimming lap pool, rated 8/10 with the caveat that real usable battery runtime at effective workout intensity is only about two hours versus the advertised 10-hour maximum.

insights.claim: iGarden's Swim Jet X Pro 10 costs $3,599 for the top-tier battery (60Ah, rated 10 hours at the lowest power setting), with a cheaper 15Ah base model starting at $1,999, positioning the product as a premium home-fitness purchase the reviewer compares to several years of gym membership costs.
insights.speaker: Christopher Null
insights.so_what: Establishes a price-performance benchmark for at-home resistance-pool fitness hardware, relevant for evaluating the home-fitness equipment category where device cost is justified against recurring gym-membership fees.
insights.quote: running a jaw-dropping $3,599 for this top-end unit. Even the cheapest model—with one-quarter the battery capacity—is $1,999

insights.claim: Actual usable battery runtime drops sharply at power levels needed for real exercise: the advertised 10-hour runtime is only achievable on the slowest, least effective setting, while the power level the reviewer found ideal for a real workout yielded just two hours of use.
insights.speaker: Christopher Null
insights.so_what: A specific product-limitation data point, advertised runtime versus usable runtime at effective settings, relevant to evaluating marketing-claim inflation common in battery-powered fitness hardware.
insights.quote: On level 3, the system ran for only two hours in my testing, and higher power levels have an even shorter lifespan.
insights.locator: While the unit can theoretically turn out 10 hours of life

insights.claim: The reviewer's wife, an experienced lap swimmer, estimated the Swim Jet workout delivers about 80 percent of the intensity of real lap-pool swimming, because the device lets swimmers stay stationary against resistance rather than requiring them to move their body through space.
insights.speaker: Christopher Null
insights.so_what: Quantifies the fitness product's efficacy gap versus the activity it substitutes for, a relevant data point for assessing product-market fit and likely customer satisfaction in the home-fitness-hardware category.
insights.quote: a full workout with the Swim Jet was about the equivalent of 80 percent of a lap-lane workout
insights.locator: As for the workout, it's legit
